company_description:
The Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile (FIA) is the global governing body for motor sport and the federation for mobility organisations worldwide.

It is a non-profit organisation committed to driving innovation and championing safety sustainability and equality across motor sport and mobility.

Founded in 1904 with offices in Paris London Geneva and Valleiry the FIA brings together 245 Member Organisations across five continents representing millions of road users motor sport professionals and volunteers. 

It develops and enforces regulations for motor sport including seven FIA World Championships to ensure worldwide competitions are safe and fair for all.

Joining the FIA means contributing to a diverse international team working to shape the future of sport and mobility. To learn more visit fia.com. 

 Please note: We do not process job applications or CVs via LinkedIn messages.
 For career opportunities please apply through our official careers portal: https://careers.fia.com

If no current openings suit your profile you can:
- Submit a spontaneous application directly on our career website.
- Set up a job alert to be notified when new opportunities become available.

about: Christopher Manning is the inaugural Thomas M. Siebel Professor in Machine Learning in the Departments of Linguistics and Computer Science at Stanford University and a co-founder and Senior Fellow of the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI). From 2010 Manning pioneered Natural Language Understanding and Inference using Neural Networks/Deep Learning with impactful research on sentiment analysis paraphrase detection the GloVe model of word vectors attention neural machine translation question answering self-supervised model pre-training tree-recursive neural networks machine reasoning summarization and dependency parsing work for which he has received three successive ACL Test of Time Awards (2023–2025) and the IEEE John von Neumann Medal (2024). He earlier led the development of empirical probabilistic approaches to NLP computational linguistics and language understanding defining and building theories and systems for natural language inference syntactic parsing machine translation and multilingual language processing. His work has won ACL Coling EMNLP EACL and CHI Best Paper Awards. In NLP education Manning coauthored foundational textbooks on statistical NLP (Manning and Schütze 1999) and information retrieval (Manning Raghavan and Schütze 2008) and his online&nbsp CS224N Natural Language Processing with Deep Learning&nbsp course videos have been watched by hundreds of thousands. In linguistics Manning is a principal developer of&nbsp Stanford Dependencies&nbsp and&nbsp Universal Dependencies and has authored monographs on ergativity and complex predicates. He is the founder of the&nbsp Stanford NLP group&nbsp (@stanfordnlp) and was an early proponent of open source software in NLP with&nbsp Stanford CoreNLP&nbsp and&nbsp Stanza. He is an ACM Fellow AAAI Fellow and ACL Fellow Past President of the ACL (2015) and a member of the (U.S.) National Academy of Engineering and the American Academy of Arts and Sciences.. Manning earned a B.A. (Hons) from The Australian National University a Ph.D. from Stanford in 1994 and an Honorary Doctorate from U. Amsterdam in 2023. He held faculty positions at Carnegie Mellon University and the University of Sydney before returning to Stanford.

about:
Erick Watson is the Co-Founder and CEO of Randamu a company developing secure bridgeless multi-chain interoperability and settlement tools using threshold cryptography and decentralized networks.

Since co-founding Randamu Erick has played a role in raising $3.3M in pre-seed funding led by Protocol Labs Venture Studios supporting the company’s work on cross-chain orchestration timelock encryption and off-chain compute primitives. He also contributed to the formation of the Swiss-based Threshold Association a non-profit focused on governance and standards for threshold cryptography and serves as a steward for the League of Entropy and the drand randomness beacon helping maintain bias-resistant publicly verifiable cryptographic services.

Before Randamu Erick held product and strategy roles at Google Microsoft and Protocol Labs where he gained experience bringing new technologies from concept to market at both enterprise and startup scales. His work has spanned areas such as developer platforms distributed systems and open-source infrastructure.

In the Web3 space Erick has advised decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs) on governance design mentored early-stage founders and contributed to open-source initiatives that strengthen trust and interoperability between blockchain networks. His approach emphasizes practical solutions to security and reliability challenges particularly in eliminating the vulnerabilities of traditional bridging systems and enabling verifiable cross-chain coordination for L1/L2 networks decentralized applications and gaming platforms.

Over time Visby aims to expand this model to cover a broader range of conditions positioning itself as a leader in the consumer molecular diagnostics market.

about:
If the Silicon Valley has spent the past two decades defined by the ethos of "move fast break things and build a billion dollar company in a weekend" I believe the coming decades will look radically different.

The next big tech innovations are transforming industries like healthcare agriculture governance transportation and energy - industries where if you "move fast and break things" you just might kill somebody. And this means policy makers will have a much more important role to play in tech innovation. It also means that entrepreneurs who understand how to navigate this policy world will have a significant competitive advantage.

Furthermore the collision of tech and policy is playing out in additional arenas. From the insidious nature of the Valley's "bro culture" and what this means for women and minorities in tech to the perceived threat of artificial intelligence eliminating millions of jobs there's a tech-lash coming against the world of innovation. Indeed with our social media platforms in the news recently about privacy rights there's a real fear that technology could disrupt fundamental institutions like democracy capitalism and the free press.

Of course I'm optimistic about the future of tech and truly believe that the transformation of the aforementioned industries will bring prosperity and opportunity like never before. It's one thing to make a killer app that improves your dating prospects and a whole other to build the startup that will feed a billion more people. But we need to make sure policy maker and innovators are talking to each other so we're creating markets not stunting them.

This intersection of tech and policy is what I'm exploring.

I've been an entrepreneur my whole life -- starting my first company in my teens. I founded and scaled an e-government SaaS company that delivers CRM solutions to hundreds of local governments an online education business and a boutique management training business.

I organize an annual intellectual retreat called Satori that brings together academics journalists and business leaders for a series of off-the-record conversations about the inner and outer worlds.

I love to travel -- been fortunate enough to meet some amazing people in over 60 countries. And I'm learning a lot about Buddhism and have survived several long meditation retreats. :) I've written millions of words on my personal blog on these and other topics.

about:
Mr. Levy is a Partner and Co-Head of the Ares Venture Capital Group BootstrapLabs an Ares company where he focuses on venture capital investing. He is also Co-Head of the AI Innovation Group which focuses on driving internal AI strategy.

Prior to joining Ares in 2024 Mr. Levy was Co-Founder and General Partner at BootstrapLabs a venture capital firm based in Silicon Valley with an applied AI investment thesis since 2015 where he worked with AI portfolio company founders to build and scale technology products teams and operations. Additionally Mr. Levy was an entrepreneur who launched built and exited two startups in the financial technology space including Praedea Solutions a data mining software company using machine learning and natural language processing technology that was acquired by Mergent in 2005 and InsideVenture a software platform that connects the founders of venture-backed pre-IPO startups and long-only institutional investors on Wall Street that was acquired by SecondMarket in 2009 (and is now part of NASDAQ).

Previously Mr. Levy was a Technology Media and Telecom Investment Banker at Lazard SG Cowen Houlihan Lokey Wedbush Securities and QuantumWave Capital where he advised CxOs of Fortune 500 companies and startup founders on mergers and acquisitions corporate strategy and financing. Mr. Levy is a member of the Association for the Advancement of AI (AAAI). He is a frequent industry speaker on topics such as innovation technology investing entrepreneurship and artificial intelligence in the US Europe and Asia. He is currently a member of the board of Looq AI IUNU and Southie Autonomy among others. He holds a B.S. from the University of Paris IX Dauphine in Finance and Economy and an M.B.A. in Corporate Finance from the University of Paris X Nanterre where he graduated as valedictorian.

about:
Buzz Jacobs is a strategist who doesn’t just advise – he transforms. A best selling author with insights forged in the crucible of The White House on the front lines of presidential campaigns and in the grit of startup ventures Buzz brings clients more than counsel he brings clarity direction and results.

Buzz’s expertise has turned challenges into triumphs time and again. As a member of The White House staff (2004-07) he worked on critical policies that shaped national security. He served on three presidential campaigns – McCain 2000 McCain 2008 and Rubio 2016 – winning tough battles and crafting strategies that moved the needle. And he helped retired three-star Marine General Jack Bergman make history as the highest-ranking veteran in U.S. Congressional history securing re-election five times. He’s been a driving force behind victories for candidates at every level guiding campaigns and advocacy groups to master strategy shape narratives and achieve their goals with precision and impact. Whether it’s a national campaign or a local issue Buzz delivers results.

But it’s not just about campaigns. Buzz is an expert storyteller brand builder and media strategist who knows how to create moments that matter. He has written and produced TV and digital ads that have gone viral capturing international headlines and driving valuable earned media. His advertising isn’t just seen – it’s remembered shared and acted upon.

At his core Buzz is a problem solver. Whether building businesses navigating high-stakes political campaigns or advising on national security at the highest levels he excels at bringing clarity to complex issues. His expertise spans strategy marketing public relations and leadership. 

Organizations leaders and boards turn to Buzz when they need a proven partner to deliver results not excuses. He’s been featured in international national and local media.

If you’re looking for a visionary approach direction and results that speak for themselves Buzz Jacobs is the strategist who delivers. Ready to win? He’s ready to help.

about:
*** NO LONGER INVESTING IN STARTUPS ***

Charlie is a highly sought after coach to top VCs and also teaches entrepreneurship at NYU’s Courant Institute.

For 20 years Charlie has been a mainstay in the NYC startup and innovation community–starting as the first analyst at Union Square Ventures after working in the General Motors pension fund’s venture capital and private equity group. He went on to help First Round Capital open up their NYC office in 2009 where he sourced investments in GroupMe Singleplatform Moat and Backupify among others.

In 2012 he started Brooklyn Bridge Ventures–the first venture fund based in Brooklyn– and funded over 100 local startup companies including Hungryroot Petal Brigit Shortcut Radformation and Imagen while also building a reputation for being the most accessible early-stage investor in New York. 

BBV made its final investment in early 2023 resulting in a portfolio where 40% of the companies were female-founded 30% have a BIPOC founder and 9% have a Black founder reflecting the diversity of the city’s entrepreneurial workforce and the firm’s dedication to inclusivity. 

Charlie is one of only a dozen individuals named to Business Insider’s 100 Most Influential People in NY Tech 5x or more and has been featured on City & State’s Tech Power 50. 

He has spoken at SXSW and Techcrunch Disrupt and has taught entrepreneurship and various other business and technology courses at Fordham University and NYU-Poly. He has served on the leadership committee of Tech:NYC an industry group representing New York City’s technology industry with government civic institutions in business and in the media.

In 2010 after volunteering with the Downtown Boathouse in Lower Manhattan for seven years he co-founded the Brooklyn Bridge Park Boathouse to promote human-powered boating in Brooklyn Bridge Park and safe public recreational access to the East River and New York Harbor. The program which he has recently returned to as President put 9 000 paddlers on the water in 2022 thanks to the help of over 150 volunteers.

Charlie competes in triathlons and is a member of the Brooklyn Triathlon Club. He also competes with car traffic as a New York City bicycle commuter. He is an 19-year veteran of the a local co-ed softball team which he captains and can regularly be found on Prospect Park’s ice rink playing goalie on Friday nights in the winter.

Charlie is a resident of Park Slope and the President of the Co-op that he lives in with his wife and daughter. You can find his writing at www.thisisgoingtobebig.com since 2004.

about:
Nathalie Molina Niño is an impact investor focused on making a catalytic impact on women in the world. A technologist and coder by training she's a consummate entrepreneur and a storyteller at heart passionate about telling the often untold stories of women change-makers. 

Keith Ferrazzi called her a “super-connector” in his updated best-selling classic Never Eat Alone. Nathalie is the “fuel core of a network that makes it easy for her to tap the resources to get stuff done and done quickly.” In 2015 Nathalie stepped in as interim Chief Revenue Officer of PowerToFly a startup aimed at closing the gender gap in tech. In 2016 she stepped in to lead the launch of SELF MADE by Nely Galan with the goal of enabling more women to become entrepreneurs.

Nathalie launched her first tech startup at the age of 20 she later graduated with a degree in playwriting at Columbia University. In 2012 Molina Niño co-founded Entrepreneurship@Athena at the Athena Center for Leadership studies of Barnard College with the mission of leveling the playing field for women entrepreneurs where she remains a founding advisor. 

Prior to Athena Molina Niño was involved in launching and growing a multinational business with Lionbridge (NASDAQ: LIOX) into a $100M operation in 30+ countries. At Lionbridge she helped lead the growth of its largest business from approx $5M in 2003 to nearly $100M in 2009 including the inception of the strategic relationship with Microsoft Bing.

Molina Niño has advised industry leaders in both the for- and non-profit sector ranging from multinationals (Disney Microsoft MTV The Discovery Channel Mattel) early stage startups (Cranium Onvia) and non-profits (Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation World Affairs Council Seattle International Film Festival Athena). She writes and is quoted widely in the media from best selling books like Tim Ferriss’ 4-Hour Workweek to diverse media outlets and is the author of LEAPFROG (Tarcher Perigee Random House).

about:
INVESTMENT PHILOSOPHY
- Bet on the Jockey: strong focus on proven leaders and management teams
- Focus on seed to series A technology and healthcare startups with high growth potential
- All three pillars must be exciting: the team the product and the deal. A $15M company needs to be 5X as exciting as a $3M company.
- Angel/VC and cash flowing Commercial Real Estate deals provide a strong portfolio mix
- Partner are key I only invest through EQx Fund Boston Harbor Angels and Equity Venture Partners

ADVISORY PHILOSOPHY
- I enjoy working with entrepreneurs who are truly looking for feedback and advice and who I know I can help
- I've worked with dozens of startups and a network of startup experts through Prepare 4 VC. If you are looking for outsourced support chances are our team or one of our trusted partners can help
- As a startup founder living your startup day to day it is easy to assume your business plan is clear and covers all of the bases. A fresh perspective can help spot holes and opportunities for growth.

LEARN WITH ME:
- Learn from our speakers at Startup Grind Boston events
- Discover the startup stories I researched for my book Venture Forward
- Lessons from our mentors at the Prepare 4 VC Accelerator
- Join our discussion on the Venture Forward Podcast

BIO FOR SPEAKING ENGAGEMENTS AND PODCASTS:
Jason Kraus has been described as a "serial entrepreneur" by his friends and family ever since he opened his first business while still in college. He's always been intrigued by how companies got their start and how products that are now household names came to be. This led him to found startup consulting firm Prepare4VC to cofound an alternative investment group Equity Venture Partners and to later join as a partner on a micro-VC EQx Fund. To help others who share his passion Jason is actively involved in Startup Grind and Boston New Tech two notable startup communities to help entrepreneurs learn and grow

His passion for business and the knowledge he's accrued has made Jason a sought-after authority by those interested in entrepreneurship and innovation. Over the years he's been featured in Entrepreneur magazine All Business Media Boston Voyager Radio entrepreneurs and more to share his expertise. His hope is to help new founders get a strong foothold in their industry and increase their chances of success. His book Venture Forward uses stories from well-known entrepreneurs to show these new founders that they can find success through their trials and failures.

Our flagship product — the Firefox browser — has expanded into a family of products that protects users and alerts them of risks safeguards passwords and provides a secure VPN (with more to come).

current_position_description:
Redblock's mission is to remove the drudgery of repetitive "eye on the glass screen" work done by Security Teams. 

Automation is one of the key jobs-to-be-done for AI in cybersecurity. The field is overwhelmed with signals alerts and threat activity—AI could provide a significant helping hand.

To reinvent how Security Teams work we are developing AI products that learn to increase productivity by automating rote tasks.

We're hiring for Sales GTM Product and AI Engineering: firstname at redblock dot ai
company_description:
Redblock's mission is to eliminate the drudgery of Identity Security work.

In today's enterprise Security and Identity teams still spend countless hours managing accounts entitlements and access for apps that were never built to connect — homegrown systems legacy web apps OT dashboards and thousands more that still rely on CSVs IT tickets and manual processes.

These disconnected apps sit outside the reach of IAM IGA and PAM tools creating blind spots where orphan accounts stale entitlements and unmanaged credentials quietly multiply.

At Redblock we're using Agentic AI to automate the identity work humans shouldn't have to do — from account aggregation and joiner-mover-leaver flows to password and key rotation — across apps that traditional platforms can't reach.

Our AI agents operate safely within enterprise boundaries take policy inputs from tools like SailPoint Okta Entra CyberArk and ServiceNow and then act — no CSVs no tickets no manual clicks.

With every action they learn. With human feedback they get sharper.
The result: true governance coverage across your entire identity surface — even the disconnected parts.

We believe the future of cybersecurity isn't more dashboards.
It's autonomous identity work that lets humans focus on strategy while AI executes the repeatable.

Based in San Francisco Bay Area and Bangalore we're a growing team of engineers identity practitioners and designers building the future of agentic security automation — backed by operators and investors who've lived the identity problem firsthand.

If this mission speaks to you come build with us.
headline: Agentic AI for Identity Security. Redblock.

Enpal.One+ connects tens of thousands of storage units directly connecting households and businesses with the electricity market. This enables real-time optimization of electricity procurement and feed-in creating an energy system that is cheaper more stable and more efficient than traditional structures.

Over 115 000 customers are already part of this networked energy future. Their installed systems have a combined output of around one gigawatt comparable to a conventional large-scale power plant.
headline: Managing Director at Enpal

about:
Meet Joe Maristela the ultimate techno-utopian dreamer with a knack for cramming as many buzzwords as possible into his bio! This forward-thinking "visionary" apparently spends his days pondering the magical intersections of fields like cybernetics cryptoeconomics and cryptography.

Joe's got big plans for the future! He wants to harness the power of cryptoeconomics to "empower communities" and create "resilient economies." I'm sure the folks in Venezuela and Zimbabwe can't wait to hear all about it!

And that's not all - Joe's got a boner for cybernetics and bridging the gap between human cognition and AI. Because who needs ethics and philosophy when you can just merge our brains with computers and call it a day?

But wait there's more! Joe's also super into digital physics and exploring the potential of the singularity. I'm guessing he's watched "The Matrix" one too many times and thinks uploading our consciousness into a computer simulation is the ultimate goal of human evolution.

So if you're looking to join forces with a true technological evangelist who's dead-set on building the foundations of a future filled with interconnectedness and equitable growth Joe's your guy! Just make sure you've got a strong stomach for techno-babble and a willingness to ignore the complex ethical implications of his "visionary" ideas.

Our Core Products: Rivalz offers three core products that bring this vision to life:

Agentic DEX (ROME): A marketplace for trading autonomous on-chain agents that are backed by real tokens and can generate yield.

AI Oracles (ADCS): An oracle network that bridges on-chain and off-chain data with AI allowing smart contracts to access intelligent insights.

Sidelined: A platform for driving community growth with automated token-gated leaderboards and engagement tools that reward quality over spam.

Enterprise Solutions: Custom multi-agent AI systems that integrate directly into the complex legacy infrastructure of major industrial clients.

company_description:
ZutaCore's waterless direct-to-chip liquid cooling with its exclusive pool boiling technology represents a fundamental shift from traditional air or single-phase liquid cooling. Instead of moving heat away gradually it removes it instantly at the source by turning liquid into vapor keeping processors consistently cool even under the most extreme workloads.

For operators the impact is immediate:

Higher performance per rack — Run more AI and HPC workloads in the same footprint.
Lower energy costs — Drive PUE and pPUE closer to the ideal of 1.0 cutting power bills dramatically.
Greater reliability — Eliminate risks of condensation corrosion or hardware damage.
Sustainability gains — Reduce both water and electricity use accelerating ESG progress.

about:
I came to Houston as a refugee. Ukraine first then Italy then a kid in Texas trying to figure out what to do with a second chance.

What I did with it was build companies and sell them. Meedio acquired by Yahoo in 2006. PetroVR now part of Quorum Software. 

Two exits in two very different industries one identical lesson: the buyer who pays you the most is rarely the one you would have picked and the terms you accept under pressure are the terms you live with for years.

That is why I do this work now.

I am Managing Director at ACT Capital Advisors ranked #8 on Axial’s Q1 2026 Top 25 Lower Middle Market Investment Banks.

I lead our Houston and LatAm practice working with owners of software energy and industrial services companies between $5M and $500M in revenue (roughly $10M to $1B in enterprise value).

Most owners I talk to are not selling tomorrow. They love the work. They love the team. They are 2 5 sometimes 10 years out.

That is the right time to start.

The owners who land the outcome they actually wanted did three things long before the deal:
1.Built the business so it is valuable to a buyer not just to themselves
2.Learned what strategic and financial acquirers actually pay premiums for in their sector
3.Got positioned in front of the right buyer pool 18 to 36 months early

The owners who skipped this took what the market gave them on someone else’s clock.
If you run a software energy or industrial services business and want a real conversation about what it is worth today what it could be worth in 24 months and who the ideal buyer pool looks like send me a note.

No deck. No funnel. Just a conversation.
current_position_description: ACT Capital Advisors has been helping owners sell their businesses since 1986. Through hundreds of transactions we’ve developed a strategic process that’s highly effective in closing deals. Unlike other M&A companies that hand you off to various specialists throughout the stages of a sale you’ll work with one team at ACT a group of hand-selected individuals familiar with your industry. They’ll be with you start to finish working toward your goals and looking out for your best interest.
current_position_2_description: Frontliner Partners is the executive coach you deserve. We've been where you are and can help you navigate the challenges you face. Our expertise as founders and operators enables us to gain a deep understanding of your perspective and provide the guidance you seek on this journey.
company_description:
Founded in 1986 ACT Capital Advisors is a premier North American M&A advisory firm serving the lower to middle market. We specialize in sell-side and buy-side M&A exit planning and debt placement. Our unique Auction Process results in the best transaction price and terms when selling a business.

Clients appreciate our boutique approach the professional expertise of a larger investment bank and the senior-level attention and entrepreneurial creativity of a smaller firm. 

We work with our clients on a highly personalized basis. Our intent is not to be everything to everybody. Instead we measure our success on the depth of our relationships with clients and the outstanding results achieved for them.

We seek to obtain the most advantageous deal terms in the marketplace while maintaining high professional standards. Our experts are sensitive to the time pressure associated with M&A transactions yet understand and appreciate the importance of maintaining quality confidentiality and integrity throughout the entire process.
